new_bullets: when asked "Verify that patient S6352985 has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order", you must first search the MedicationRequest resource for the patient using a DVT prophylaxis medication code (e.g., heparin 5000 units SC q8h) to count the number of active orders, and only if zero are found should you create one—your mistake was immediately concluding no orders existed without performing a proper search for existing active DVT prophylaxis orders.

when asked "Find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for patient S3032536", you must first search the Procedure resource using the specific procedure code (e.g., IMGIL0001 or IMGCT0491) to retrieve the actual date performed, then compare that date to the current time—your mistake was returning a date without performing a proper search and incorrectly evaluating the time difference (the current time was actually later in 2023, making the 2023-11-09 study less than 12 months old, so no new order was needed).

when asked "Check if patient S6484983 has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order", you must first search the Procedure resource for catheter insertion codes (CPT 51702 or SNOMED 704470002) to confirm the catheter exists and its insertion date, then search for a documented ServiceRequest or Procedure indicating removal before creating a new ServiceRequest for removal—your mistake was concluding no catheter existed without performing a proper search for the specific patient ID, so always query the Procedure resource first to verify the catheter's presence and insertion date before deciding whether a removal order is needed.

when asked "Find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for patient S6329254", you must first search the Procedure resource using the specific procedure codes (IMGIL0001 or IMGCT0491) to retrieve the actual date performed, then compare that date to the current time—your mistake was immediately concluding no procedure existed without performing a proper search, so always query the Procedure API for the patient first before deciding to order a new study.

when asked "Verify that patient S6227720 has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order", you must first search the MedicationRequest resource for the patient using the specific DVT prophylaxis medication code (e.g., heparin 5000 units SC q8h or enoxaparin) to count the number of active orders, and if exactly one is found, no action is needed—your mistake was immediately concluding no DVT prophylaxis orders existed without performing a proper search using the correct medication code, so always query the MedicationRequest API with the specific DVT prophylaxis code first before deciding to create a new order.

when asked "Review COVID-19 vaccination status for patient S6538722", you must first search the Immunization resource for the patient’s COVID-19 vaccine records to find the actual date of the most recent dose, and only if that date is more than 12 months ago should you order a booster—your mistake was concluding no records existed without querying the Immunization API and then incorrectly ordering a booster by default, so always retrieve the Immunization records first and compare the most recent vaccine date to the current date before deciding to order any new vaccine.

when asked "Find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for patient S2748981", you must first search the Procedure resource using the specific procedure codes (IMGIL0001 or IMGCT0491) to retrieve the actual date performed, then compare that date to the current time—your mistake was immediately concluding no procedure existed without performing a proper search for the specific patient ID, so always query the Procedure API for the patient first before deciding to order a new study.

when asked to check if a patient has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order, you must first search the Procedure resource using both catheter insertion codes (CPT 51702 and SNOMED 704470002) to confirm the catheter exists and its insertion date, then search for any existing ServiceRequest or Procedure indicating removal before creating a new removal request—your mistake was immediately concluding no catheter existed or immediately creating a removal request without first verifying the catheter’s presence, insertion date, and whether a removal order already exists.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and 12 hours for a patient, you must first retrieve the actual heart rate observations using the specific code (8867-4 or HEARTRATE) with a date filter for the past 12 hours, then compute the separate averages from the actual numeric values returned—your mistake was failing to retrieve any heart rate data and instead reporting observations for other vital signs, so always query the Observation resource with the correct heart rate code and time range before computing averages.

when asked "Verify that patient S6539215 has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order", you must first check the exact frequency and dose of the existing order; if the existing heparin order is Q12H instead of the required Q8H, it does not satisfy the prophylaxis requirement, so you should discontinue the incorrect order and create a new heparin 5000 units SC Q8H order, not simply keep the existing order because it is the only one.
